channel.title: Latest Finextra Research Artificial intelligence Headlines
channel.description: The Latest Financial IT Artificial intelligence Headlines

item.title: Indonesia's Pintarnya raises $16.7m
item.description: Pintarnya, an Indonesian platform that helps users find jobs and also offers access to secured loans, has raised $16.7 million in Series A funding.

item.title: Meta inks £10 billion cloud deal with Google
item.description: Alphabet’s Google cloud computing services have been tapped by Meta in a deal worth at least $10 billion, a part of Meta’s move to expand AI capabilities.

item.title: CommBank reverses plan to replace call centre staff with AI
item.description: Commonwealth Bank of Australia has rowed back its decision to cut 45 contact centre jobs after finding that the AI-powered "voice-bot" designed to reduce calls actually led to an increase in work for humans.

item.title: Kraken acquires Capitalise.ai
item.description: Crypto exchange Kraken has acquired Capitalise.ai, an Israel-based supplier of no-code, natural-language trading and analytical tools.

item.title: Grasshopper Bank adopts Narmi's MCP server for AI business insights
item.description: Digital business bank Grasshopper has moved into a private beta of Narmi's Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, laying the groundwork for client queries via Anthropic's AI assistant Claude.

item.title: Fi Money's 'Magic Lens' gives users a true picture of their net worth
item.description: Indian money management firm Fi Money is giving users a comprehensive overview of their financial net worth by snapping photos of physical assets and providing an instant valuation.

item.title: Casca raises $29m for AI-based loan origination platform
item.description: AI-native loan origination platform Casca has raised $29 million in a Series A funding round led by Canapi Ventures.

item.title: Robinhood UK rolls out AI investing assistant to decode market movements
item.description: Robinhood is rolling out an AI-powered investing assistant to help UK users interpret and navigate market movements.

item.title: BBVA and the University of Navarra embark on applied research project into responsible AI
item.description: BBVA is entering a three-year project with the University of Navarra to address the main ethical, technical and regulatory challenges posed by artificial intelligence.

item.title: CommBank joins OpenAI goldrush
item.description: Australia's CommBank has joined the rush of financial institutions to form a relationship with OpenAI, striking a multi-year deal to provide employees with access to ChatGPT Entperise.

item.title: Santander partners OpenAI to become 'AI-native' bank
item.description: Santander has inked a deal with OpenAI, rolling out ChatGPT Enterprise to nearly 15,000 staffers as it bids to become an "AI-native" bank.

item.title: LHV streamlines legal document processing with AI from Luminance
item.description: UK bank LHV is to apply artificial intelligence technology from Luminance to streamline legal contract document workflows.

item.title: Standard Chartered and Ant International extend AI collaboration effort
item.description: Standard Chartered and Ant International today announced a new collaboration to introduce an AI-Powered Treasury and FX Management Solution that integrates Ant International’s Falcon Time-Series Transformer (TST) Model with Standard Chartered’s Aggregated Liquidity Engine (SCALE).

item.title: Zil Money adds AI-powered virtual card features
item.description: Zil Money, a leading fintech solution, has introduced two innovative features in its Virtual Card suite: AI-powered receipt parsing and automated spending analysis reports.

item.title: Citi gives commercial banking platform an AI makeover
item.description: Citi’s single-entry point digital client platform, CitiDirect Commercial Banking, continues to be rolled out globally, and now has additional enhanced functionality, leveraging the power of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to achieve client experience efficiencies as well as streamlined digital enhancements.

item.title: Financial crime detection platform Ivix raises $60 million
item.description: IVIX, the first AI-powered platform designed to help governments around the world combat financial crime at scale using LLMs, advanced graph analytics and publicly available data, today announced the close of its $60 million Series B funding round, bringing the total amount raised to $85 million.

item.title: Stripe to tap pent up demand for stablecoins and AI in Asia
item.description: Stripe, the programmable financial services company, today showcased stablecoin and AI product updates at Stripe Tour Singapore 2025 to accelerate global growth for Asian businesses.

item.title: Eltropy platform adds integrations
item.description: Eltropy, the leading AI-powered conversations platform for community financial institutions (CFIs), today announced that its unified offering, Eltropy One Platform, now features over 50 integrations with essential systems used by credit unions and community banks.

item.title: BBVA rolls out global data plaform to Mexico and Colombia
item.description: BBVA has taken a significant step forward in its data-driven transformation strategy with the successful implementation of ADA, its global data platform, in Mexico and Colombia.

item.title: Anna Money proves LLM/agent capability in UK company registration
item.description: With agentic AI emerging as the next big shift in technology, ANNA Money is proud to announce that it has successfully proven its ability to work directly with Large Language Model (LLM) agents to complete one of the most complex, highly regulated onboarding processes in UK business, company registration with full authorisation, identity verification and payment.

item.title: FNZ rolls out Advisor AI
item.description: FNZ, the global wealth management platform, has today announced the global launch of FNZ Advisor AI, a generative AI solution embedded directly into FNZ’s market leading wealth management platform.

item.title: PowerPay partners with Synovus Bank and Nearwater Capital
item.description: PowerPay, a leading fintech platform providing consumers with home improvement and patient finance, today announced the addition of Synovus Bank, a Georgia-based financial institution with $62 billion in assets under management, to its committed warehouse lending syndicate. The Company is also pleased to share that Nearwater Capital, a leading New York-based provider of asset-based financing solutions, will be providing Risk Retention Financing to help support the Company's securitization program. The addition of these two partners significantly expands PowerPay's capital structure, enhancing the Company's capacity to bolster the rapid growth of its lending program, which has become one of the largest integrated consumer finance platforms in the U.S.

item.title: Worldpay and Trulioo collaborate on safeguards for agentic AI commerce
item.description: Worldpay, a global leader in payment technology, today announced they are partnering with Trulioo, the leading global digital identity platform, to introduce new safeguards for AI-powered agent-led commerce.

item.title: Spektr upgrades compliance software with AI Agent Builder
item.description: spektr, the compliance infrastructure platform trusted by leading fintech innovators, announces the launch of spektr 2.0 – a modular reimagining of compliance architecture built for flexibility and speed.

item.title: How Key Trends Are Redefining Wealth Management in 2025
item.description: Attending the Communify Insights Experience in London, Rupert Baron, Former CEO, Investment Management, Rathbones joined the FinextraTV studio and gave his perspective on what 'wealth' actually means in an industry of growing complexity. Defining wealth as a merge of investment and financial planning, Barron provided overviews on consolidation, private equity and AI, what it is that has driven such change, and how firms can move forward in the modern era.

item.title: How to Turn Data Into Insight in a New Era of Financial Intelligence
item.description: Stepping away from a busy event, Leon Saunders Calvert, President & Managing Director - B2B, The Economist Group sat down with FinextraTV to discuss the role of data in a new era of financial intelligence. Describing how bigger data isn't as good as qualified data, Sanders Calvert helps to contextualise some of the key considerations when interoperating data in the current industry landscape.

item.title: How the City of London Is Growing Its Identity as a Global Fintech Hub
item.description: Attending the Communify Insights Experience in London, Howard Dawber, Deputy Mayor for London, Business & Growth joined the FinextraTV studio to share details on London's identity as a growing hub for Fintech and how it compares to other global cities.

item.title: Wealth Management: Both Easier & More Complex Than It Ever Was
item.description: Attending the Communify Fincentric Experience 2025, Michael Smith, EVP - Business Development, Communify Fincentric spoke to FinextraTV about the seemingly conflicting outlook of the current wealth management industry. In some sense, Smith believes, there are more tools to make the process easier but, at the same time, it is harder than ever to be successful. Diving into client requirements and the definition of a good product, Smith provides his expert analysis of the situation.

item.title: The Importance of Events: Trust, Partnership & Education
item.description: Joining the FinextraTV studio at the Communify Fincentric Experience, Michael Rodgers, EVP - Events, Communify Fincentric explains the benefits of an industry event and how they provide a strong, trustworthy space to connect with clients and suppliers alike, as well as informing, education and inspiring. Using AI as an example of the overwhelming market of information, Rodgers describes how events have a special way of cutting through that noise and complexity.

item.title: How to Handle AI Scepticism as a Banking Leader
item.description: Speaking to FinextraTV, Runki Goswami, Global Marketing Head & CMO at Newgen Software, and Varun Ghai, Associate Vice President (P&S), UK & EU, Newgen software discuss the use of AI and technology in banking against a backdrop of heightened customer needs. Explaining the different elements that are now available to banks, Goswami and Ghai also help to define what good leaders look like in this new era of technological change and scepticism.

item.title: The 3 Most Compelling AI Use Cases in Wealth Management
item.description: As part of our new Future of Wealth Series, Joe Bonanno, Global Head of Data Analytics & Innovation - Citi Wealth, Citi joined FinextraTV to discuss some of the key components of the current wealth management sector. Specifically, Bonanno brings insights into how AI is being receieved across the industry and the ways in which it is particularly useful in relation to data inputs for large language models, task optimisation and research.

item.title: How AI Can Reduce Decision Fatigue for Asset Managers
item.description: Joining the FinextraTV Virtual Studio, Phil Cox, COO, Russell Investments and Roshan Shetty, BFSI & Public Sector Head - Americas, Tech Mahindra discuss the decision fatigue within asset management currently and how AI helps alleviate that. Splitting the task abilities that can be improved into analysis, portfolio construction, risk management and trading strategies, Shetty and Cox debate the ease if AI adoption into existing processes and how information-based tools have risen to the top.

item.title: AI & Wealth: How to Build Trust Through Impact & Personalisation
item.description: Whilst at Money 20/20 in Amsterdam, Bahadir Yilmaz, Chief Analytics Officer, ING joined FinextraTV to provide some data insights in relation to growing AI conversations. Describing the challenge of taking data from its origins into actionable, presentable insights for clients, Yilmaz explains how a succesful, easy process will help to increase client's comfort with sharing larger, useful portions of data. Yilmaz, while speaking to AI use cases, says that clients are not interested in what happens behind-the-scenes, they care more for faster services and experiences where they can personally notice the change.

item.title: Australian Wealth: How Superannuation Growth is Prompting Technology Conversations
item.description: Whilst at the Communify Fincentric Experience 2025, Lachlan Hardy, Head of Sales & Business Development - APAC, Communify Fincentric helped to provide his unique insight into how the Australian wealth market compares to others. Speaking to the growth of self-directed wealth and superannuation funds, Hardy describes how one of the key considerations are around how technology is interacting and enabling this growth.

item.title: How A2A Innovation is Advancing The Fight Against Fraud
item.description: Speaking to FinextraTV in this interview, Helena Forest, EVP, Global Product & Commercial, Real Time Payments, Mastercard and Luke Reynolds, SVP, Financial Crimes, Mastercard discuss the impact of fraud and payment scams and look to address their prevention. As well as covering the many forms of fraud, such as APP fraud and romance scams, Forest and Reynolds explore the innovations currently available to help reduce its impact. They provide an honest optimism whilst remaining aware of the existing complexities within fraud prevention, bringing attention to the impact of the latest account to account fraud prevention methods including risk-scoring and Mastercard's UK A2A Sandbox.

item.title: How Partnerships Can Give Banks More Technological Agility
item.description: At the FinextraTV studio during Temenos Community Forum 2025, Monty Bhatia, EVP, Global Alliances and Partner Ecosystem, Temenos and Sudip Lahiri, Executive Vice President & Head - Europe & UKI, Financial Services, HCLTech spoke about core modernisation and AI implementation. Explaining the benefits of working with trusted partners, Lahiri and Bhatia explored how banks can effectively transition to an agile, modular architecture that enables fast implementation of AI-driven services.

item.title: How Rip-And-Replace Alternatives Help to Reimagine Banking
item.description: Sitting down with FinextraTV, Keith Redding, CRO, Universal Banking, Finastra discussed what it means to Reimagine Banking and why having the right technology is crucial in this new world. As well as providing real-world examples, Redding describes how the future is more about progressive transformation than 'rip and replace' and how this approach can help banks turn challenges into opportunities.

item.title: Modernising for what’s next: Data-led innovation in financial services
item.description: AI is here – but is the industry’s data ready?

item.title: What’s next in wealth? Exploring European trends & priorities
item.description: How can wealth managers best serve the next generation of investors?

item.title: Growing pains: Evolving core banking for an age of AI
item.description: From aging foundations to digital-first banking and AI.

item.title: How smart FIs are achieving their AI transformation targets
item.description: Discover the most successful AI use cases in banking.

item.title: GenAI and data management: The next-generation approach to customer experience
item.description: Why should FIs implement GenAI across their data processing flows?

item.title: Consumer lending’s future: When embedded finance meets smarter decisioning
item.description: What role does AI-driven decisioning play in enabling smarter, fairer, and faster credit approvals? How can the industry ensure transparency and accountability in automated lending?

item.title: Banking AI Implementation Case Study: Design of a Living Bank User Experience
item.description: What if your digital bank wasn’t just a service, but a living companion? Over six months, we designe...

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Manual Payments and How AI-Driven Automation is Reshaping Finance
item.description: Your finance team spends countless hours on tasks that shouldn't exist. Every day, they manually ma...

item.title: Causal AI: Going Beyond Correlation in Finance
item.description: Today, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become a buzzword across industries. In finance, AI is used...

item.title: The big picture keeps getting bigger – all the way to Quantum-safe Trust Infrastructure
item.description: I began posting here years ago about payment automation and e-banking. Then about connecting bank cu...

item.title: Quantum Machine Learning and Portfolio Optimization: The Next Frontier of Smart Investing
item.description: Introduction: The Portfolio Optimization Process Needs to Be Revamped. For decades, portfolio optimi...

item.title: Sponsor's message: [Webinar] Navigating Banking Platform Modernisation: How to Fuse Reliability with Innovation
item.description: [Webinar] Navigating Banking Platform Modernisation: How to Fuse Reliability with Innovation
